reasoning: The question requires the number of 'Violence against civilians' events in CAR to reach 10 times the baseline 30-day average over the prior 360 days. With the known baseline at ~32 events, the threshold requires 330+ events in a single 30-day period. This equates to an average of 11 separate recorded attacks on civilians per day, an incredibly intense rate of violence that would make major international headlines. Searches reveal no signs of an unprecedented massacre campaign or structural surge in January/February 2026. While post-election tensions exist from late December 2025, they have not triggered anything near this magnitude. Furthermore, if ACLED were to integrate a new data source with micro-events, it would typically back-date them throughout the year, which would raise the 360-day baseline, thus also increasing the absolute requirement for the 10x threshold. Therefore, reaching this massive multiplier is highly improbable. I estimate minimum allowed probabilities, slightly increasing for the farther horizon.
